ddls:
CREATE TABLE "incidents" (
  "incident_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each incident */,
  "incident_date" TEXT /* Date of the incident */,
  "incident_time" TEXT /* Time of the incident */,
  "location" TEXT /* Location of the incident */,
  "incident_type" TEXT /* Type of incident (e.g., officer-involved shooting, use of less-lethal force) */,
  "incident_description" TEXT /* Brief description of the incident */,
  "incident_category" TEXT /* Category of the incident (e.g., violent crime, traffic stop) */,
  "incident_severity" TEXT /* Severity level of the incident (e.g., low, medium, high) */,
  "incident_status" TEXT /* Status of the incident (e.g., open, closed, pending) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("incident_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "subjects" (
  "subject_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each subject */,
  "race" TEXT /* Race of the subject */,
  "sex" TEXT /* Sex of the subject */,
  "age" INTEGER /* Age of the subject */,
  "has_injury" INTEGER /* Whether the subject was injured during the incident */,
  "height" TEXT /* Height of the subject */,
  "weight" TEXT /* Weight of the subject */,
  "occupation" TEXT /* Occupation of the subject */,
  "address" TEXT /* Address of the subject */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("subject_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "officers" (
  "officer_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each officer */,
  "name" TEXT /* Name of the officer */,
  "badge_number" TEXT /* Badge number of the officer */,
  "department" TEXT /* Department the officer belongs to */,
  "rank" TEXT /* Rank of the officer */,
  "years_of_service" INTEGER /* Number of years the officer has been in service */,
  "training" TEXT /* Training received by the officer (e.g., crisis intervention, de-escalation techniques) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("officer_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "incident_officers" (
  "incident_id" INTEGER /* ID of the incident */,
  "officer_id" INTEGER /* ID of the officer */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the officer in the incident (e.g., primary officer, secondary officer) */,
  "officer_action" TEXT /* Action taken by the officer during the incident (e.g., fired a warning shot, used pepper spray) */,
  "officer_injury" INTEGER /* Whether the officer was injured during the incident */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("incident_id", "officer_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "incident_subjects" (
  "incident_id" INTEGER /* ID of the incident */,
  "subject_id" INTEGER /* ID of the subject */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the subject in the incident (e.g., suspect, victim) */,
  "subject_action" TEXT /* Action taken by the subject during the incident (e.g., resisted arrest, complied with orders) */,
  "subject_injury" INTEGER /* Whether the subject was injured during the incident */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("incident_id", "subject_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "resistances" (
  "resistance_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each resistance */,
  "incident_id" INTEGER /* ID of the incident */,
  "type" TEXT /* Type of resistance encountered (e.g., physical, verbal) */,
  "resistance_description" TEXT /* Brief description of the resistance encountered */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("resistance_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_resistances_incident_id FOREIGN KEY ("incident_id") REFERENCES incidents ("incident_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "force_continuum" (
  "force_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each force level */,
  "incident_id" INTEGER /* ID of the incident */,
  "level" TEXT /* Level of force used (e.g., deadly force, less-lethal force) */,
  "force_description" TEXT /* Brief description of the force used */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("force_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_force_continuum_incident_id FOREIGN KEY ("incident_id") REFERENCES incidents ("incident_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "injuries" (
  "injury_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each injury */,
  "incident_id" INTEGER /* ID of the incident */,
  "type" TEXT /* Type of injury sustained (e.g., gunshot wound, blunt force trauma) */,
  "injury_description" TEXT /* Brief description of the injury sustained */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("injury_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_injuries_incident_id FOREIGN KEY ("incident_id") REFERENCES incidents ("incident_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "name" TEXT /* Name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., officer, analyst, admin) */,
  "department" TEXT /* Department the user belongs to */,
  "permissions" TEXT /* Permissions assigned to the user (e.g., view incidents, edit incidents) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)
column_names: Type of incident (e.g., officer-involved shooting, use of less-lethal force)
